name: Ace of Swords
uprightMeaning: Mental clarity, breakthrough, truth, and the swift, decisive cut of a mind finally aligned with its purpose.
reversedMeaning: Confusion, miscommunication, a brutal truth wielded without wisdom, or clarity that has not yet arrived.

name: Two of Swords
uprightMeaning: Indecision, stalemate, avoidance, and the blindfolded paralysis that comes when two equally weighted options demand a choice.
reversedMeaning: Facing the truth, a decision finally made, or removing the blindfold to see what was always there.

name: Three of Swords
uprightMeaning: Heartbreak, sorrow, grief, and the piercing clarity that comes only when illusions are finally cut away.
reversedMeaning: Recovery, healing after sorrow, releasing grief, or the slow return of wholeness following deep pain.

name: Four of Swords
uprightMeaning: Rest, recovery, contemplation, and the necessary stillness that restores what conflict and exertion have depleted.
reversedMeaning: Restlessness, burnout from refusing to rest, or slow re-emergence from a period of necessary isolation.

name: Five of Swords
uprightMeaning: Conflict, defeat, hollow victory, and the bitter taste of winning at the cost of something that cannot be reclaimed.
reversedMeaning: Reconciliation, releasing the need to win, or moving beyond a conflict that has already been survived.

name: Six of Swords
uprightMeaning: Transition, moving on, calmer waters ahead, and the quiet exodus from turbulence toward a horizon of peace.
reversedMeaning: Resistance to change, unfinished personal work, or returning to turbulent waters before healing is complete.

name: Seven of Swords
uprightMeaning: Strategy, deception, cunning, and the calculated act of taking only what can actually be carried to safety.
reversedMeaning: Coming clean, ending deception, or confronting a pattern of evasion that has gone on too long.

name: Eight of Swords
uprightMeaning: Restriction, self-imposed limitation, mental imprisonment, and the startling truth that the blindfold can be removed at any time.
reversedMeaning: Freedom, reclaiming power, seeing through self-imposed restriction, or finally stepping out of a comfortable cage.

name: Nine of Swords
uprightMeaning: Anxiety, nightmares, despair, and the insistent midnight voice that amplifies fears beyond their actual proportion.
reversedMeaning: Releasing anxiety, finding perspective, healing after a difficult mental period, or the first light after dark nights.

name: Ten of Swords
uprightMeaning: Painful endings, betrayal, defeat, and the absolute rock-bottom that paradoxically holds the seed of a new dawn.
reversedMeaning: Recovery, refusing to stay down, or the rising that follows the most complete collapse.
